operating_state: Total sales increased 12% to $11.9 billion driven by higher end-user demand and dealer inventory builds of $700 million versus $100 million prior year. Consolidated profit reached $1.531 billion, up 40%, with operating margin expanding to 15.3% from 13.2% as higher volumes offset increased expenses. Growth occurred across all segments with Asia/Pacific recovery led by China, strong performance in Latin America and EAME, while North America remained flat. Cash increased to $11.342 billion from operational cash generation of $1.928 billion.
cost_structure: Manufacturing costs increased primarily from $300 million reinstated short-term incentive compensation versus zero prior year, partially offset by favorable material costs and lower warranty expense. Operating cash flow improved to $1.928 billion from $1.130 billion through better working capital management. Inventory increased to $12.149 billion reflecting preparation for demand recovery and providing manufacturing leverage on higher production volumes.
material_events: Acquired Oil & Gas division of Weir Group for $356 million net of cash in February 2021. Issued $500 million ten-year bonds at 1.9% and redeemed $500 million 2.6% notes due 2022 to optimize capital structure. Elected Gerald Johnson to Board effective March 1, 2021. Maintained quarterly dividend at $1.03 per share with no share repurchases.
macro_exposures: Monitoring semiconductor, transportation, and raw material supply chain disruptions with contingency plans developed. Tracking pandemic acceleration in overseas markets and associated cost pressures, though no significant operational impacts occurred. Standard exposures continue to global economic conditions, commodity price fluctuations, international trade policies, and ongoing COVID-19 effects.
forward_outlook: Expect $400 million restructuring costs in 2021 with $150 million incremental benefit from prior actions. Short-term incentive compensation projected at $1.3 billion for full year. Capital expenditures projected at $1.2 billion with pension contributions of $310 million. Do not expect significant dealer inventory increases beyond first quarter.
uncertainty_profile: Supply chain contingency planning ongoing for semiconductors, transportation, and raw materials amid fluid conditions. Credit facility covenants maintained with consolidated net worth at $16.7 billion above $9.0 billion requirement. Financial Products segment faces typical customer financing credit risks while COVID-19 pandemic continues creating uncertainty regarding business disruption duration and geographic spread.
